item.text: I’ll inspect the vulnerability note first, then trace the affected code paths before editing so the fix stays scoped to the reported issue.

item.text: The vulnerable path is `--exec`: it builds a single shell command from user templates and video metadata, then runs it with `shell=True`. I’m checking the existing tests and option semantics so the remediation preserves documented shell-command support while preventing metadata from becoming shell syntax.

item.text: The template engine already has a `%(... )q` conversion that shell-quotes values, but `--exec` currently does not require or default to it. I’m looking at the formatter internals to add shell quoting after normal field formatting, which keeps width/default/replacement behavior intact.

item.text: I noticed a subtle formatter edge case: precision/width could be applied after quoting, which can truncate quote characters. I’m tightening the implementation so normal formatting is applied first, then the completed field value is shell-quoted as a whole.
I’m checking the patched block immediately because generator comprehensions with conditional iterables are easy to get syntactically wrong.

item.text:
Implemented the remediation for the reported command injection path.

Changed:
- [yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py](/work/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py:1257): added opt-in `shell_quote_fields` handling for output templates.
- [yt_dlp/postprocessor/exec.py](/work/yt_dlp/postprocessor/exec.py:12): `--exec` template substitutions now shell-quote metadata fields before running through `shell=True`.
- [test/test_postprocessors.py](/work/test/test_postprocessors.py:94): added regression coverage for shell metacharacters in substituted fields.

Verified with:
- `python -m py_compile yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py yt_dlp/postprocessor/exec.py test/test_postprocessors.py`
- `python -m unittest test.test_postprocessors`
- `python -m unittest test.test_YoutubeDL test.test_utils.TestUtil.test_shell_quote`

`pytest` is not installed in this environment, so I used the project’s unittest-compatible test path.
